message:
feat: add synchronous package resource resolution for config fast paths

Adds PackageManager::resolve_package_resources_blocking() — a best-effort
synchronous resolver that returns resolved package resource paths when
all packages are already installed locally, without spawning a tokio
runtime or performing any install/update work.

When a package source (npm or git) has not been installed yet (i.e., the
install directory does not exist), the method returns Ok(None) to signal
that the caller should fall back to the full async resolve() path which
can install missing packages.

This enables the config --show/--json fast paths introduced in the
previous commit to check whether packages contribute any config sections
without paying the async runtime startup cost when packages are already
present locally.

Implementation mirrors the async resolve() logic but runs entirely on
the calling thread: reads settings snapshots, deduplicates package
sources, resolves local/npm/git paths, and accumulates resource paths
into a ResolvedPaths struct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
